complaint_text: I noticed three transactions on my Visa credit card totalling $4,280 that I did not authorise. The charges were made at electronics stores in Sydney while I was overseas. I contacted my bank immediately but they refused to reverse the charges, claiming I must have shared my PIN.

My variable home loan rate was increased by 0.50% without adequate notice. The bank claims they sent a letter but I never received it. My monthly repayments went up by $320 which is causing financial hardship. The Product Disclosure Statement says 30 days notice is required.

My home was damaged in the January floods. I lodged a claim for $45,000 in repairs but the insurer denied it saying the damage was caused by 'rising water' not 'storm damage' and therefore excluded under my policy. I believe this interpretation is unreasonable as the water entered through the roof during the storm.

I was approved for a $25,000 personal loan despite having existing debts of $60,000 and a net income of only $3,200 per month. The lender did not verify my expenses or existing obligations. I am now in severe financial hardship and cannot meet repayments.

I have been trying to locate my superannuation account from a previous employer for 18 months. The fund claims they have no record of my membership despite me having statements from 2018. I estimate approximately $32,000 should be in the account.

I was charged a $450 annual fee on my platinum credit card despite calling to downgrade my card two months prior. The bank has no record of my call and refuses to refund the fee. I specifically remember the call as I was advised the downgrade would take effect immediately.

I lost my job due to company restructuring and requested a 3-month repayment pause on my home loan. The bank refused and instead offered to extend my loan term by 5 years which would cost me an additional $85,000 in interest. I believe this is not a genuine hardship arrangement as required under the National Credit Code.

My car was written off in an accident on 15 November 2022. It has been over 2 months and my insurer has not yet assessed my claim. I am paying for a rental car at $65 per day. The PDS states claims will be assessed within 10 business days.

My bank closed all my accounts without explanation, giving me only 30 days notice. I have been a customer for 15 years with no issues. When I asked for a reason they cited 'commercial decision'. This has disrupted my direct debits and salary deposits causing significant inconvenience.

My income protection claim was denied after 8 months of being on claim. The insurer arranged an independent medical examination and the doctor said I could return to work despite my treating specialist disagreeing. I have chronic back pain and cannot sit for more than 30 minutes.

I purchased a laptop online for $2,100 but received a counterfeit product. I requested a chargeback from my bank but they declined saying I needed to resolve the dispute with the merchant first. The merchant is based overseas and is unresponsive.

I refinanced my home loan to another lender and requested discharge of my existing mortgage. It has been 6 weeks and the bank has not completed the discharge despite the legal requirement to do so within 14 days. My new lender is charging a higher rate until settlement.

After a fire damaged my home, I discovered I was underinsured by approximately $200,000. My insurer had not contacted me to review my sum insured for 5 years despite significant construction cost increases. The insurer has an obligation under the Insurance Contracts Act to clearly inform me of underinsurance risk.

I repaid my fixed rate personal loan early and was charged $3,200 in break costs. The loan contract does not clearly disclose how break costs are calculated. I believe the calculation method is unreasonable and not compliant with ASIC guidelines on early termination fees.

My Total and Permanent Disability claim through my superannuation fund has been in assessment for 14 months. I have provided all requested medical evidence but the fund keeps requesting additional reports. I have not been able to work since my accident and am relying on Centrelink.

I have been overcharged interest on my credit card for an estimated 6 months. The bank applied the cash advance rate of 21.99% to regular purchases instead of the purchase rate of 17.99%. When I raised this they acknowledged the error but only refunded 3 months of overcharges.

My mortgage broker recommended a loan product with a 2-year honeymoon rate that then reverted to a rate significantly higher than the market average. The broker received a higher commission on this product. I believe the broker failed to act in my best interests as required under the National Consumer Credit Protection Act.

My home insurance premium increased by 42% at renewal with no explanation. When I called the insurer they said it was due to 'increased natural disaster risk' but could not provide specific details. I have never made a claim in 12 years of being with this insurer.

I was scammed into transferring $18,500 to a fraudulent investment platform. I reported the scam within 2 hours but my bank says they cannot recover the funds. I believe the bank should have had better fraud detection systems and should have flagged the unusual transaction pattern.

My life insurance claim was denied due to alleged non-disclosure of a pre-existing condition. I disclosed my full medical history at the time of application and answered all questions truthfully. The insurer is relying on a GP visit from 2015 where I mentioned occasional headaches.

My 125,000 reward points valued at approximately $625 were expired without adequate notice. The bank claims they sent an email notification but I never received it. The terms state points expire after 36 months but I believe the notification was insufficient under consumer law.

My offset account balance of $85,000 was not correctly offsetting my home loan interest for approximately 8 months due to a system error. The bank acknowledges the error but is only offering to refund $2,400 in overcharged interest. My calculations show the overcharge is closer to $3,800.

My travel insurance claim for $8,500 in medical expenses was denied because the insurer says my pre-existing asthma was not disclosed. I declared my asthma on the application form and paid an additional premium for coverage. I have the receipt confirming the additional premium payment.

A debt collector has been calling me 3-4 times daily and has also contacted my employer. The debt relates to a personal loan that I have been disputing as the amount is incorrect. The collector's conduct breaches ACCC debt collection guidelines and is causing me significant distress.

I requested to switch my superannuation investment option from High Growth to Cash 3 weeks ago due to market volatility concerns. The fund has not processed my switch and the market has dropped 8% in that time, resulting in an estimated loss of $12,000.

My credit card limit was increased from $8,000 to $15,000 without my request or consent. I have a history of financial difficulty and had previously asked the bank not to increase my limit. This increase has led to additional debt of $5,000 that I cannot afford to repay.

My bank arranged a property valuation for my refinance application that came in at $680,000 - significantly below recent comparable sales of $780,000-$820,000 in my area. As a result my LVR is too high for the product I applied for. I believe the valuation is inaccurate and biased.

My insurer offered a cash settlement of $22,000 for my written-off vehicle which I believe is worth at least $28,000 based on comparable listings. The insurer used automated valuation data that does not account for the low kilometres and excellent condition of my vehicle.

My bank's mobile app and internet banking were down for 3 days during which I could not access my accounts or make payments. This resulted in two direct debit dishonours costing me $60 in fees from billers and a late payment mark on my credit report.

My income protection claim for anxiety and depression has been denied because the insurer says my condition does not meet the policy definition of 'total disability'. My psychiatrist has certified I am unable to work in any capacity but the insurer's medical assessor disagrees after a 30-minute phone assessment.

I transferred $12,000 to my credit card on a 0% balance transfer offer for 18 months. After 3 months the bank started charging 19.99% interest claiming I made a purchase on the card which voided the promotional rate. This condition was buried in fine print and not clearly disclosed.

My construction loan progress payment of $85,000 has been delayed by 6 weeks despite my builder meeting all requirements. The delay has caused my builder to stop work and is costing me $500 per week in rental accommodation. The bank cannot explain the reason for the delay.

I cancelled my car insurance policy mid-term and was charged a $300 cancellation fee plus a short-rate penalty that reduced my refund by 40%. The PDS mentions a cancellation fee but does not disclose the short-rate calculation method. I expected a pro-rata refund.

I was asked to be a guarantor for my son's $40,000 business loan. The bank did not properly explain my obligations or the risks involved. My son has defaulted and the bank is now pursuing me for the full amount. I was not given independent legal advice as recommended by the Banking Code.

My partner passed away and the superannuation fund is distributing the death benefit to his ex-wife instead of me. We have been in a de facto relationship for 8 years and I am listed as the nominated beneficiary. The fund says the nomination had lapsed.

My credit card was skimmed at a petrol station and $6,800 in fraudulent transactions were made over 3 days. I reported the fraud immediately upon discovering it but the bank is holding me liable for $2,000 claiming I was negligent in protecting my card details. I dispute this finding.

I requested to switch from a fixed rate to variable rate home loan at the end of my fixed term. The bank delayed processing my request by 3 weeks during which I was charged the revert rate of 6.5% instead of the variable rate of 5.2%. This cost me approximately $1,100 in excess interest.

My building insurance claim for subsidence damage was denied under a 'earth movement' exclusion. However the subsidence was caused by a burst water main under my property which is covered under 'escape of liquid'. The insurer is applying the wrong exclusion.

My bank sent my complete financial statements including account balances and transaction history to another customer with the same surname. This is a serious privacy breach under the Privacy Act. I have suffered distress knowing a stranger has access to my financial information.

My husband's life insurance claim was denied under the 13-month suicide exclusion clause. However my husband died in a single vehicle accident and the coroner's report made no finding of intentional self-harm. The insurer has no evidence to support their claim of suicide.

I paid $3,500 for furniture that was never delivered. The merchant has gone into liquidation. I requested a chargeback from my bank within the Visa timeframes but they are refusing to process it saying they need proof the merchant cannot deliver. The merchant is in liquidation - that is the proof.

I was verbally promised a rate lock on my home loan application but this was not documented. Between application and settlement the rate increased by 0.25%. The bank denies any rate lock was agreed. I have a text message from the loan officer confirming the rate would be held.

My insurer arranged repairs to my water-damaged kitchen but the workmanship is substandard. The new cabinetry is misaligned, the benchtop has visible seams, and the plumbing is leaking again. I have had 3 trades back to fix issues and the work is still not acceptable.

The advertised comparison rate on my personal loan was 8.5% but the actual total cost when including ongoing fees is equivalent to 12.3%. The lender's advertising is misleading and does not comply with ASIC comparison rate requirements under the National Credit Code.

My financial adviser switched my superannuation from a low-cost industry fund to a retail fund with higher fees without explaining the fee difference. My annual fees went from $680 to $2,400. Over 5 years this will cost me approximately $8,600 more in fees plus lost investment returns.

Someone opened a credit card in my name using stolen identity documents and accumulated $15,000 in charges. The bank approved the application without adequate identity verification. I have a police report and have notified IDCARE. The bank is still pursuing me for the debt.

My home loan settlement was delayed by 2 weeks due to the bank's internal processing errors. I had to pay penalty interest to the vendor of $4,200 and lost my preferred property inspection period. The bank has offered only $500 in compensation which is grossly inadequate.

My insurer has assessed my flood-damaged vehicle as a total loss and offered $35,000 market value. The vehicle is a modified 4WD with $12,000 in aftermarket accessories that were declared to the insurer. The insurer is refusing to include the accessories in their valuation.

My business overdraft facility was reduced from $100,000 to $50,000 without adequate notice during a period of seasonal cash flow shortage. This has caused me to dishonour payments to suppliers and is threatening the viability of my business. The bank gave only 7 days notice.

I cancelled my life insurance policy within the 14-day cooling off period but have not received my refund of $1,200 after 6 weeks. The insurer keeps saying it is being processed but cannot provide a timeline. I am entitled to a full refund under the Insurance Contracts Act.

I was charged a foreign transaction fee of 3.5% on purchases made in Australian dollars through international websites. The bank's PDS states the fee only applies to transactions in foreign currency. The merchant processed in AUD so no currency conversion occurred.

I was approved for a $750,000 home loan that I clearly cannot afford based on my income of $85,000. The bank did not properly assess my expenses or existing HECS debt. I am now in arrears and facing potential property loss. The bank failed its responsible lending obligations.

My neighbour's tree fell on my fence and carport during a storm causing $15,000 damage. My insurer says I need to claim against my neighbour's policy. My neighbour is uninsured. I believe my home and contents policy should cover this as storm damage to my property.

Despite having an active financial hardship arrangement my lender has continued to report negative information to credit bureaus and has engaged a debt collector. This is contrary to the hardship arrangement terms and ASIC guidelines on financial hardship obligations.

I have been paying for duplicate life insurance through two superannuation accounts for 4 years without being notified. Under the Protecting Your Super legislation the fund should have consolidated my accounts or at least notified me. I have paid approximately $4,800 in unnecessary premiums.

My credit card payments are being allocated to the lowest interest rate balance first instead of the highest. Under ASIC credit card reforms from 2019, payments above the minimum must be allocated to the highest interest rate balance. My bank is not complying with this requirement.

I provided a family guarantee for my daughter's home loan. She has defaulted and the bank is demanding I sell my home to cover the guarantee. The bank did not explain the risks adequately and I was not advised to seek independent legal advice. I am 72 years old and this is my only asset.

My insurer has not responded to my 6 phone calls and 3 emails over 4 weeks regarding my burglary claim. I have been without essential items including my laptop which I need for work. The lack of communication is causing significant stress and financial impact.

My bank reported a default on my credit file for a debt that was paid in full 2 months before the default was listed. I have proof of payment including bank transfer receipts. The incorrect default is preventing me from obtaining a home loan.

My trauma insurance claim for a heart attack was denied because the insurer says my cardiac event does not meet their specific medical definition of 'heart attack' which requires a certain level of troponin elevation. My cardiologist confirms I had a myocardial infarction requiring emergency surgery.

I purchased credit card debt protection insurance and am unable to work due to injury. My claim under the debt waiver was denied because the insurer says my condition was pre-existing. I developed the condition 3 years after purchasing the insurance and it was not pre-existing at the time of purchase.

My bank has restricted access to my home loan redraw facility of $45,000 without notice. The funds represent additional payments I have made over 5 years. The bank says they are reviewing my account but will not provide a timeframe for releasing the funds. I need the funds for medical expenses.

My insurer is deducting 30% from my claim for roof replacement due to 'betterment' claiming my roof was old. My policy is a 'new for old' replacement policy and there is no betterment clause in my PDS. The insurer cannot unilaterally apply betterment deductions not specified in the policy.

I cancelled a personal loan within the cooling off period but the lender retained establishment fees of $800 and interest charges. Under the National Credit Code I am entitled to a refund of all fees and charges paid minus any credit fees or charges that are government fees or charges.

My superannuation fund's marketing materials showed historical returns of 9.5% p.a. but my actual returns over 3 years have averaged only 4.2%. The fund's advertising used a different investment option's performance data to the option I was enrolled in. This is misleading conduct.

I was charged cash advance fees and interest from the date of withdrawal for an ATM transaction at my own bank's ATM. The transaction should have been processed as a normal savings withdrawal from my linked account but was incorrectly processed as a credit card cash advance.

I paid $12,000 in Lenders Mortgage Insurance when I took out my home loan. I refinanced after 2 years and was told LMI is non-refundable. I believe I am entitled to a partial refund as the insurance period is typically for the life of the loan and I have substantially reduced the risk.

My home suffered a burst pipe causing flooding to 3 rooms. My insurer took 4 days to authorise emergency make-safe repairs during which time the water damage spread significantly. The additional damage cost an extra $12,000 to repair which the insurer is now disputing.

My bank is withholding $28,000 from my merchant facility due to an excessive chargeback ratio. However the chargebacks are from a single fraudulent customer and I have provided evidence of delivery for all disputed transactions. The bank is causing severe cash flow problems for my small business.

My ex-husband changed the beneficiary on his life insurance policy 2 days before his death. The change was made while he was in hospital under heavy medication. My children should be the beneficiaries as per the original nomination. I question his capacity to make the change.

My credit card loyalty program terms were changed reducing the earn rate from 1.5 points per dollar to 0.75 points. The bank gave 30 days notice but I had specifically chosen this card for the earn rate and pay a $395 annual fee. I believe I should be released from my annual fee obligation.

My home loan application has been in processing for 8 weeks with no decision. I have provided all requested documentation promptly. The delay has caused me to miss the sunset date on my property purchase contract and I have lost my $25,000 deposit.

My flood damage claim was denied because the insurer says the water entered my property from a creek overflow which is defined as 'flood' and excluded. However the water first entered through storm-driven rain before the creek overflowed. I believe the primary cause was storm not flood.

My personal loan lender continued to direct debit my account after I cancelled the arrangement and provided a new payment method. Three unauthorised debits of $580 each were taken causing my account to go into overdraft and incur $90 in fees.

I requested a rollover of $120,000 from one super fund to another. The money left my old fund 6 weeks ago but has not arrived at my new fund. Both funds say the other is responsible. In the meantime I have missed 6 weeks of investment returns on a significant sum.

I gave my adult son a supplementary credit card with a $2,000 limit. He spent $8,000 by requesting and being granted a temporary limit increase without my knowledge. The bank should not have increased the limit on a supplementary card without the primary cardholder's authorisation.

I am selling my home and my bank is charging a $28,000 fixed rate break cost. The break cost calculation appears to use a wholesale rate that does not align with publicly available swap rates. I have asked for a detailed calculation but the bank has refused to provide one.

My insurer wants to repair my accident-damaged vehicle rather than writing it off. The repair quote is $18,000 which is 75% of the vehicle's market value. I believe the vehicle should be written off as the structural integrity cannot be guaranteed after such extensive repairs.

I need to access my $50,000 term deposit early due to medical emergency. My bank is charging an early withdrawal penalty of $2,800 which I believe is excessive. Given the medical circumstances I believe the bank should waive or significantly reduce the penalty under their hardship policy.

My income protection claim was denied because the insurer says my disability commenced during the 90-day waiting period. My medical evidence shows symptoms began after the waiting period ended. The insurer is relying on a single GP note that mentions 'initial symptoms' during the waiting period.

I applied for financial hardship assistance on my credit card and was offered a reduced minimum payment of $100 per month. However the bank is still charging the full interest rate of 21.99% which means my balance is increasing despite making payments. This is not genuine hardship assistance.

My ex-partner took out an additional $50,000 on our joint home loan without my knowledge or consent. The bank processed the increase based on a forged signature. I am now liable for a debt I did not agree to. The bank failed to properly verify my consent.

My business interruption insurance claim relating to COVID-19 government restrictions was denied. Following the AFCA test case ruling my policy should cover these losses as it does not contain a specific pandemic exclusion. The insurer is not following the test case determination.

My personal loan contract does not include all required information under the National Credit Code including the total amount of interest payable and the comparison rate. I believe the contract is non-compliant and I should not be liable for interest charges above the disclosed amount.

My mother's binding death benefit nomination was rejected by the superannuation fund because it was witnessed by a fund employee instead of two independent witnesses over 18. The fund should have informed my mother that the nomination was invalid when it was lodged 3 years ago.

My credit card was stolen and used for 47 contactless transactions totalling $4,700 before I noticed and reported it. The bank is holding me liable for all transactions under $100 saying I should have reported the theft sooner. I did not know the card was stolen until I checked my statement.

I am moving interstate and my bank agreed to port my home loan to a new property. After I committed to purchasing the new property the bank withdrew portability approval citing a change in lending criteria. I am now facing two mortgages and significant financial stress.

My insurer is charging me three separate excesses of $750 each for a single storm event that caused damage to my roof, fence and carport. The damage resulted from the same weather event and I believe only one excess should apply as per the policy terms.

I transferred $15,000 AUD to a family member overseas and was charged a margin on the exchange rate that resulted in the recipient receiving $800 less than expected. The bank's disclosed fee was $25 for the transfer but the exchange rate margin was not transparently disclosed.

My life insurance premium has increased by 85% over 3 years from $1,200 to $2,220 per year despite no change in my circumstances. The insurer attributes the increase to 'portfolio experience' but cannot explain why my individual premium has jumped so significantly.

I accumulated $12,000 across four buy now pay later accounts despite earning only $42,000 per year. None of the providers conducted meaningful credit assessments. I am now struggling to meet repayments on all accounts simultaneously and am in financial hardship.

My new offset account was not linked to my home loan for 3 months due to a bank processing error. During this time my $95,000 in savings was not offsetting my loan and I was overcharged approximately $1,900 in additional interest. The bank is only offering to refund $1,200.

My landlord insurance claim for $22,000 in tenant damage was denied because the insurer says I did not conduct property inspections every 3 months as required by the policy. I conducted inspections every 4 months which I believe is reasonable. The damage occurred between inspections.

My lender unilaterally changed the terms of my personal loan increasing the administration fee from $10 to $15 per month. The contract states fees may be varied but does not specify the mechanism or limits. I believe unilateral fee increases without negotiation are unfair contract terms.

My application for early release of superannuation on compassionate grounds for medical treatment has been delayed by 8 weeks. My surgery is urgent and I cannot afford to wait. The fund is requesting documentation that I have already provided twice.

My 12-month interest-free period on a $5,000 purchase expired and I was retrospectively charged interest on the full purchase amount of $1,100. I was not given adequate notice that the interest-free period was about to expire. One email notification buried in marketing material is insufficient.

My mortgage broker is demanding I pay a $3,500 clawback fee because I refinanced my home loan within 2 years. The broker's agreement mentions a clawback clause but I was told verbally this would not be enforced. The clawback should be between the broker and lender not the consumer.

My business cyber insurance claim for a ransomware attack causing $45,000 in business losses was denied because the insurer says I did not maintain 'reasonable security measures' as required by the policy. I had antivirus software and regular backups. The insurer has not defined what additional measures were required.

My solicitor's trust account held at the bank was mismanaged resulting in a shortfall of $35,000 that was used for my property settlement. The bank allowed withdrawals that did not match the trust authorisation requirements. I believe the bank has a duty of care regarding trust accounts.

I am leaving my employer and want to transfer my group life insurance to an individual policy. The insurer is requiring me to undergo full medical underwriting despite the policy stating I can transfer without medical evidence within 30 days of leaving. I am within the 30-day window.
